{{Quote|This reminds me of Bespin. My Squadron scouted it once for a possible Imperial takeover. Too small, though...|[[Kasan Moor]]|<ref name="RS I">[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']]</ref>}}
'''Taloraan City''', also known as '''Cloud City,''' was the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Taloraan/Legends|Taloraan]] and one of the sites of the [[battle above Taloraan]]. It featured several tall, spire-like buildings atop a massive [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] generator, similar to technologies used on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]].

==History==
===Clone Wars===
Taloraan City,<ref name="Wind Raiders">''[[The Clone Wars: The Wind Raiders of Taloraan]]''</ref> sometimes referred to as Cloud City,<ref name="RS I guide">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]''</ref> was a [[City/Legends|city]] floating in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Taloraan/Legends|Taloraan]]. The city was home to a series of [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] gas [[Mining/Legends|mining]] facilities. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], it was visited by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] on a diplomatic [[Mission to Taloraan|mission]].<ref name="Wind Raiders" />

===Galactic Civil War===
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] led [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] to [[Battle above Taloraan|destroy]] most of the Imperial-owned tibanna gas in an attempt to weaken the local [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kohl Seerdon]]'s resources.<ref name="RS I" />

==Behind the scenes==
Taloraan City first appeared in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']]. ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' mistakenly refers to the structure as "Cloud City."
